source: Crowdsourced from Glassdoor India, AmbitionBox, LinkedIn Salary Insights, AngelList India, iimjobs.com, and direct founder surveys, 2024-25
note: All figures are annual CTC (Cost to Company) in INR. These are India startup-adjusted rates, not MNC/FAANG rates. ESOP ranges are % of fully-diluted equity at time of grant. Significant variation exists across companies.

roles.title: Co-Founder / CTO
roles.description: Technical co-founder or first engineering hire with ownership stake. Typically one of the original founding team members.
roles.typical_scope: Sets technical direction, architecture, hires first 10 engineers, manages all technology decisions, often still codes in early stages
roles.esop_notes: This is a founding equity stake, not an ESOP grant. Standard 4-year vesting with 1-year cliff. If joining post-founding, typical CTO equity is 3-8% at seed.
roles.hiring_tips: Look for founders with full-stack competency + product sense. Code quality matters less than system design and team scaling instinct. IIT/NIT pedigree is a signal but not mandatory — GitHub portfolio matters more.
roles.interview_signal: Can architect a system that serves 1M users from scratch AND has hired/managed at least 5 engineers before.

roles.title: VP Engineering / Engineering Head
roles.description: First engineering leadership hire post-funding. Transitions the team from founder-led to process-led engineering while keeping velocity high.
roles.typical_scope: Manages 5-20 engineers, owns technical roadmap, recruiting, architecture decisions, engineering culture
roles.esop_notes: At seed: 1-2% ESOP with 4yr/1yr cliff is market. At Series A, 0.5-1% for a proven VP Eng is competitive.
roles.hiring_tips: Look for someone who has scaled an engineering team 3-5x in the past. Avoid 'VP Eng' who's never managed more than 3 people. Ask for org chart of their best team.
roles.interview_signal: Can walk through how they debugged a production incident that cost the company money AND how they structured the hiring process for their last team.

roles.title: Senior Software Engineer
roles.description: Experienced full-stack or specialist engineer who can own entire features independently. The most critical hire for early-stage startups — one great senior SWE outperforms 3 average engineers.
roles.typical_scope: Designs and implements major product features end-to-end, mentors junior engineers, reviews PRs, participates in architecture decisions
roles.esop_notes: 0.25% at seed for a great senior SWE is competitive. Early hires (#5-15) should get 2-3x the standard rate.
roles.hiring_tips: Screen for ownership mentality — have they shipped things end-to-end before? Past startup experience is a strong positive signal. Avoid SWEs who've only worked in large-team environments where they owned one small piece.
roles.interview_signal: Has shipped a complete product feature from design to production and can explain the technical trade-offs they made.

roles.title: Software Engineer (Mid)
roles.description: Mid-level engineer who can deliver features with some guidance. Good hire for early startups who've already validated direction and need to scale development.
roles.typical_scope: Implements features from spec, fixes bugs, writes tests, works within established architecture with some independent decision-making
roles.esop_notes: Early mid-level hires (#15-30) can get 0.1-0.2%. Later hires should get 0.05%.
roles.hiring_tips: Strong DSA skills are less important than delivery velocity. Ask for 3 projects they built in the last year. Bonus if they've worked at a previous startup.
roles.interview_signal: Has shipped 3+ complete projects and can debug an issue in code they've never seen before.

roles.title: ML / AI Engineer (Senior)
roles.description: Applied ML engineer who can take a model from research to production. In the AI era, the most in-demand and hardest-to-hire engineering role in India.
roles.typical_scope: Builds and deploys ML models, manages training pipelines, owns model performance metrics, works with product to define AI features
roles.esop_notes: AI engineers command a premium on both cash and ESOP. If you're an AI-first company, budget 0.4-0.6% for your first senior ML hire.
roles.hiring_tips: Verify their deployed models vs research projects. Ask what their model achieved in production metrics vs baseline. Strong in LLMOps, vector DBs, and RAG pipelines is the 2025 requirement.
roles.interview_signal: Has taken a model from prototype to serving 10K+ requests/day in production, including monitoring, retraining, and drift detection.

roles.title: Head of Product / VP Product
roles.description: First dedicated product leader. Translates business strategy into product roadmap, manages PM team, drives OKRs and customer research.
roles.typical_scope: Owns product roadmap, manages PMs, drives customer research and discovery, works with engineering on delivery, reports to CEO
roles.esop_notes: For the founding team or first 5 employees, a VP Product who joins pre-seed can expect 1-2% founding stake. Post-funding, 0.5-1% with 4yr vesting.
roles.hiring_tips: Look for PMs who've shipped 0-to-1 products and also scaled them. The skill sets are very different — early product requires customer obsession, later requires process. At pre-seed, hire the former.
roles.interview_signal: Can describe a product they decided NOT to build, why, and what happened next.

roles.title: Senior Product Manager
roles.description: Experienced PM who can own a product area end-to-end. Often the first dedicated PM hire at a startup, working directly with the founding team.
roles.typical_scope: Owns a product squad, drives discovery and delivery, writes PRDs, manages backlog, analyzes data, coordinates with design and engineering
roles.esop_notes: First PM hire often gets 0.25-0.5% ESOP at seed stage. Subsequent PM hires get 0.05-0.15%.
roles.hiring_tips: Most important test: can they write a PRD you'd actually build from? Ask for a sample of their best PRD before the final round.
roles.interview_signal: Can walk through a feature decision they made where the data pointed one way and their intuition pointed another — and explain why they chose what they chose.

roles.title: Senior UX / Product Designer
roles.description: Full-stack designer who can handle UX research, visual design, and product prototyping. Critical hire for consumer-facing products.
roles.typical_scope: Owns design system, conducts user research, creates wireframes and high-fidelity mockups, implements design in Figma, works with engineering on implementation
roles.esop_notes: Design is often underpaid in early-stage Indian startups. A great senior designer at pre-seed who builds the design culture should get 0.15-0.25% ESOP.
roles.hiring_tips: Portfolio quality matters more than degree. Look for designers who've shipped — not just made beautiful Dribbble shots. Ask to see user research they've conducted.
roles.interview_signal: Can walk through a design decision they changed based on user feedback and quantify the impact.

roles.title: Head of Growth / VP Marketing
roles.description: Growth leader who owns customer acquisition and retention. Either performance marketing dominant (for D2C/B2C) or content/PLG dominant (for B2B SaaS).
roles.typical_scope: Owns CAC targets, manages marketing budget, leads SEO/SEM/social/content, drives PLG loops, coordinates with sales on pipeline
roles.esop_notes: Growth leaders with strong track records command premium. 0.5% at seed for a proven growth person is competitive.
roles.hiring_tips: Ask for specific channel-level CAC and ROAS numbers from their last role. Vague answers ('we grew 3x') are a red flag. Growth is a numbers game.
roles.interview_signal: Can show a specific growth experiment they ran, what the hypothesis was, what the result was, and what they'd do differently.

roles.title: Business Development Manager
roles.description: Hunter role focused on opening new accounts, partnerships, and revenue channels. Critical at Seed when you need to prove repeatable revenue.
roles.typical_scope: Prospecting, outbound, partnership development, closing deals, managing a pipeline of 30-50 accounts
roles.esop_notes: BD roles are often on variable pay. Total OTE (base + variable) is 1.5-2x base. ESOP is secondary motivation.
roles.hiring_tips: Test with a live role-play: give them a cold calling scenario and listen for their ability to handle objections. Past startup BD experience is a strong positive.
roles.interview_signal: Can name 3 deals they closed in the last 12 months, the ACV, and what the winning argument was.
roles.commission_note: Typical OTE = 1.5-2x base at seed stage. Structure: 50-60% base + 40-50% variable on revenue targets.

roles.title: Customer Success Manager
roles.description: Owns onboarding and retention for a book of business. Critical for B2B SaaS — prevents churn before it happens.
roles.typical_scope: Manages 20-50 accounts, drives onboarding, QBRs, upsell conversations, NPS, and renewal pipeline
roles.esop_notes: CSMs rarely get meaningful ESOP unless they're very early hires. First CSM (#5-10 employee) should get 0.1-0.15%.
roles.hiring_tips: Look for CSMs who track churn rate and NRR (Net Revenue Retention). Ask their best GRR and NRR numbers from their previous role.
roles.interview_signal: Can describe a customer who was about to churn, exactly what they did to retain them, and what the outcome was.

roles.title: Finance Controller / Head of Finance
roles.description: First full-time finance hire. Manages bookkeeping, compliance, MIS reporting, cash management, and audit. Critical hire before a fundraise.
roles.typical_scope: Monthly P&L, cash flow management, statutory compliance, audit coordination, investor reporting, cap table maintenance
roles.esop_notes: Finance heads in startups are often underpaid on cash and undercompensated on equity. A good finance controller pre-Series A is worth 0.2-0.3% ESOP.
roles.hiring_tips: Look for CA/CMA with startup experience. The difference between a startup-experienced finance person and a corporate finance person is significant — they should know how to work with limited resources.
roles.interview_signal: Has prepared investor-grade financial models and MIS reports before, and can work in Tally/Zoho Books without a large team.

esop_context.standard_pool_size: 10-15% pre-money at seed, 10-12% pre-money at pre-seed
esop_context.vesting_cliff: 4 years total, 1-year cliff (25% vests at 12 months, then monthly thereafter)
esop_context.esop_tax_treatment: Options taxed as perquisite at exercise (income tax at slab rate). Shares taxed on sale (LTCG at 10% if held 24+ months post exercise).
esop_context.esop_valuation: Rule 11UA valuation required by a SEBI-registered valuer every time you issue new options. Cost: ₹25,000-₹75,000 per valuation exercise.
esop_context.early_exercise: Some startups allow early exercise (exercise before vesting) to reduce the tax burden on employees — the employee pays LTCG rate on exit instead of income tax at exercise.

general_notes: All figures are annual CTC (Cost to Company), which includes basic salary, HRA, PF contribution, and other allowances. Take-home is typically 65-75% of CTC for salaried employees.
Provident Fund (PF) contribution is mandatory for salaries below ₹15,000/month basic. Many startups offer voluntary PF or structure salaries to minimize PF if the employee prefers higher take-home.
Indian startup salaries are typically 20-40% below equivalent MNC/FAANG roles. ESOP is the compensation delta — ensure ESOP vesting is clearly documented in the offer letter.
Bengaluru has the highest startup salaries due to the highest cost of living and most competitive talent market. Hyderabad and Pune are 15-20% more affordable for equivalent talent quality.
For early hires (#1-10), negotiate primarily on ESOP, not salary. A 0.5% equity stake in a company that reaches ₹500 Cr valuation is worth ₹2.5 Cr — far more than a ₹3L/year salary premium.
Notice periods in India are typically 30-90 days. Build this into your hiring timeline — a senior SWE who needs to serve 90 days notice means 3 months from offer to start.
